Committee.CommitteeName: Special Interim Committee on Property Tax Reform
Special Committee on Property Tax Reform

RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 101-J, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Tiffany Price, a Democrat, proudly represents part of Jackson County (District 26) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2024.
Rep. Price is a civic leader, mental health advocate, and nonprofit founder committed to youth empowerment and public service. She is the Executive Director of Hold Em Up 4 Care (HEU4C), a nonprofit she launched in 2018 following her completion of the Parent Leadership Training Institute (PLTI). Tiffany brings lived experience insight to her work in education, health, and community development.
She holds an Associate of Arts degree from Metropolitan Community College and is a certified Notary Public. Her credentials include Trauma-Informed Care (Alive and Well, 2024), Adult Mental Health First Aid (First Call, 2022), and Youth MHFA (Great Circle, 2023). Tiffany also serves on the Brookside Charter School Board, is a Family Ambassador at KIPP Legacy High School, and sits on the System of Care for School-Based Mental Health Steering Committee.
Tiffany is in her boy-mom era with Bryan, Phillip, Preston, and Karter. Tiffany leads with heart, purpose, and a deep commitment to equity.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Creates provisions relating to health insurance reimbursement of doula services
Creates the offense of unlawful possession of an ammunition feeding device that holds more than ten rounds
Creates provisions relating to automatic enrollment in the supplemental nutrition assistance program for certain MO HealthNet participants
Modifies provisions relating to unemployment compensation and eliminates the waiting week period requirement
Specifies that children diagnosed with certain conditions must be eligible for MO HealthNet benefits
Modifies the senior citizens property tax relief credit
Modifies provisions relating to the Missouri working family tax credit act and makes the tax credit refundable

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Lowers the age for adult high schools from 21 to 18
Establishes the "Firefighters' Procedural Bill of Rights Act"
Establishes the "Firefighters' Procedural Bill of Rights Act"
Requires bleeding control kits in all public school and charter school classrooms

Authorizes the "Child Care Contribution Tax Credit Act", the "Employer-Provided Child Care Assistance Tax Credit Act", and the "Child Care Providers Tax Credit", relating to tax credits for child care
Prohibits certain discriminatory practices on the basis of race
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
